text: The surgeon begins preparation in the midline near the umbilicus by incising the peritoneum and releasing the bladder from the ventral abdominal wall. The dissection continues towards the pubic bone and symphysis, using a monopolar scissor and PK dissector to pull the bladder down, revealing the pubic bone, symphysis, and ventral aspect of the prostate. The robotic instruments create an extraperitoneal space similar to a balloon trocar technique.

The surgeon identifies the junction between the ligamenta umbilicale medial and the vas deferens at the crossing above the iliac artery. Using a monopolar scissor and PK dissector, the iliac vein, obturator nerve, and endopelvic fascia are exposed. The remaining tissue between the lateral and dorsal aspects of the prostate is bluntly dissected, and the procedure is repeated on the contralateral side.
